description: AGENTS.md fails ruff format --check because markdown formatting is experimental in ruff preview mode. Affects all phases that run ruff format on AGENTS.md.
risk: Low for DG scope. Does not affect Python code quality or governance semantics.
follow_up: Tooling hygiene phase when ruff stabilizes markdown formatting support.
evidence: AGENTS.md passes ruff format --check --preview; ruff stable-mode failure is preview feature gate, not file defect. ruff format --diff --preview AGENTS.md: no diff, file already formatted.
notes: Closed by reclassification. AGENTS.md has no formatting defect. Ruff requires --preview for Markdown because its Markdown formatter is experimental. Future Markdown ruff checks must use --preview until Ruff stabilizes Markdown formatting. This was always a tool maturity issue, not an Ordivon file debt.

description: .coveragerc is untracked residue from a previous phase. It is present in the working tree but never staged or committed.
risk: Medium — untracked files accumulate, may accidentally be committed via blind git add. Currently excluded from all commits.
follow_up: Tooling residue triage phase — decide to commit with justification or delete.
evidence: DG-6D: .coveragerc deleted as accidental residue. Minimal 2-line coverage config for tool not in governed verification workflow.
notes: Deleted in DG-6D. If coverage tooling is needed, create with explicit governance.

description: .pre-commit-config.yaml is untracked residue from a previous phase. Present in working tree but never staged or committed.
follow_up: Tooling residue triage phase — decide to commit with justification or delete.
evidence: DG-6D: .pre-commit-config.yaml deleted as accidental residue. Hooks duplicate existing pr-fast baseline gates. References broken 'uv run'.
notes: Deleted in DG-6D. If pre-commit hooks are needed, create with explicit governance and non-broken tooling.

description: Two manifest tests (test_valid_manifest_passes, test_summary_counts_correct) are unstable in mixed governance test sessions. They pass reliably in isolation but exhibit xfail/xpass under full governance pytest due to subprocess state pollution. Underlying check_verification_manifest.py always produces correct 11/11 on real data.
risk: Medium — current DG meta-verification tests exhibit instability; could mask real regressions or erode trust in governance test suite.
follow_up: Remove xfail by isolating subprocess/env state or rewriting tests to use deterministic injected baseline data. Fix before further meta-verification expansion or Rust kernel scoping that uses these tests as parity source.
evidence: Post-DG-H1: Root cause was shallow dict() copy on VALID_MANIFEST in three negative tests (test_non_hard_gate_fails, test_noop_command_fails, test_empty_command_fails), causing shared gate dict mutation between test runs. Fixed by using copy.deepcopy(). Also eliminated subprocess from _run_checker by calling extract_baseline_gates + check_invariants directly. Tests now deterministic: 10 passed × 10 runs, 0 xfail/xpass. Full governance: 192 passed, 0 xfail/xpass. check_verification_manifest.py: 11/11. pr-fast: 11/11.
notes: Fixed in Post-DG-H1. Shallow copy mutation bug, not subprocess state pollution. Subprocess approach was collateral — now replaced with direct function calls for speed and determinism.

description: test_generator_output_deterministic is non-deterministic due to generated doc count drift. The wiki generator produces varying document counts across runs, making the output deterministic check unreliable. Not introduced by OGAP-Z. Needs deterministic source ordering / registry count normalization before future document governance hardening.
risk: Low — flaky test does not affect governance semantics or verification correctness. Pollutes 'all-green' trust signal in subsequent phases.
follow_up: Fix source ordering or registry count normalization in wiki generator to make output deterministic. Required before any further document governance hardening phase.
evidence: OGAP-Z closure: test_generator_output_deterministic FAILED (1 failed, 1256 passed). Skipped during OGAP-Z verification as pre-existing, not in OGAP-Z scope. Test passes non-deterministically depending on doc generation ordering.
notes: Known debt. Skip in verification until fixed. Do not suppress as xfail — the non-determinism is a real issue that should not be papered over.

scope: Document Governance Pack / document registry completeness / legacy identity surfaces
description: DG coverage gap: Document Governance Pack contract claims 'all project documentation under docs/' is governed, but check_document_registry.py validates only registered documents, allowing unregistered legacy AegisOS docs and identity-bearing config/package surfaces to remain undetected while governance checks pass. This is a meta-verification gap: the checker validates what it knows about, but does not verify it knows about everything it should.
risk: High — undermines governance trust. checker pass ≠ docs coverage complete. Current identity can be polluted by old config. New AI may read AegisOS/PFIOS as current identity. Future public wedge may carry old brand residue.
follow_up: Two-layer fix: (1) Identity surface hygiene — clean PFIOS/AegisOS from config/package/README/onboarding surfaces (done in PV-N2H). (2) DG coverage hardening — add document registry completeness audit for discoverable docs under docs/ not already registered or explicitly archived. Consider adding identity-surface checker for pyproject.toml, package.json, apps/*/package.json, README.md, .env.example, docker-compose.yml. Decide whether to expand DG contract scope or add separate identity-surface governance.
evidence: PFIOS/AegisOS audit discovered: pyproject.toml name='pfios', package.json name='pfios-monorepo' with 18 PFIOS_* scripts, .env header 'PFIOS Environment Configuration', apps/web/package.json name='pfios-web', apps/api/pyproject.toml name='pfios-api'. 20+ AegisOS documents under docs/product/ and docs/architecture/ were unregistered while check_document_registry.py returned PASS on 31 registered docs only. DG contract scope table explicitly excludes config files, .env, and CI YAML from governance — a contract-level blind spot for identity-bearing files.
notes: Registered during PV-N2H identity hygiene triage. Two sub-gaps identified: (A) Contract scope gap — identity-bearing config/package/env files excluded from DG scope. (B) Checker implementation gap — document registry lacks completeness check for unregistered docs. PV-N2H cleaned identity surfaces but did not close the coverage gap. Full remediation requires DG contract revision + checker completeness mode. Blocks PV-N3 public quickstart until triaged.

description: External benchmark sources must be treated as versioned references. Frontier-AI governance frameworks evolve rapidly. EGB-1 used initial historical anchors; future EGB phases must check current official versions.
follow_up: Check current versions of all 5 frameworks before EGB-2.
evidence: OpenAI PF v2 2025-04. Anthropic RSP v3.x 2026. DeepMind FSF ongoing updates. NIST RMF under revision.
notes: Post-closure source freshness audit. Low severity.
